title: Listening - Part 3: Dealing with a Problem
description: You are going to hear five extracts in which people talk about a problem they faced while travelling. For questions 19–23, choose from the list A–H what helped each speaker solve the problem. Use the letters once only. There are three letters that you do not need to use.
instructions: You are going to hear five extracts in which people talk about a problem they faced while travelling. For questions 19–23, choose from the list A–H what helped each speaker solve the problem. Use the letters once only. There are three letters that you do not need to use.

extracts.context: Five people talk about a travel problem they experienced.
extracts.audio_script: Speaker 1 Woman: My friend and I were walking in a coastal area when a path marked on our map had been closed because of fallen rocks. The alternative signs weren't clear, and our phones had no connection. [19]An elderly man working in his garden noticed us studying the map and described another route through nearby fields that took us safely back to the village.[/19] It added an hour to our walk, but we arrived before dark. Without his local knowledge, we might have gone in completely the wrong direction.||Speaker 2 Man: On my first journey abroad alone, my train was cancelled at a large station in France. The announcement was in French and I understood only that something had changed. I felt close to panic. [20]A railway employee at the information desk checked my ticket, wrote down the platform for a replacement train and explained where I would need to change later.[/20] Once I had those instructions, the journey was straightforward. Her patience mattered more than the delay, and I reached my hostel safely that evening.||Speaker 3 Woman: While travelling in the mountains, the weather changed suddenly and my walking group had to spend longer outside than intended. It became cold and wet very quickly. [21]Fortunately, before leaving, our leader had made everyone pack waterproof clothing, extra food and a torch, even though the morning was sunny and some of us said it was unnecessary.[/21] We reached shelter without any real danger. The experience taught me that preparations can seem excessive until the exact moment you need them.||Speaker 4 Man: I left my small bag in a café while visiting Madrid and only noticed when I was several streets away. It contained my passport, so I ran back feeling desperate. I had studied Spanish at evening classes, although I was never confident using it. [22]I managed to explain to the waiter in Spanish what the bag looked like and where I'd been sitting, and he immediately brought it from behind the counter.[/22] I was extremely relieved, and proud that the language I'd learned had proved genuinely useful.||Speaker 5 Woman: We had planned to drive to a music festival, but halfway there the car began making a strange noise and a mechanic told us not to continue. At first we thought the weekend was ruined. [23]Instead of giving up, we left the car for repair, took a train to a different town and spent the weekend exploring museums and restaurants there.[/23] We missed the bands, naturally, but we had a surprisingly good time. Being willing to abandon the original plan saved the trip from becoming a complete disappointment.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_1
dialogue.text: My friend and I were walking in a coastal area when a path marked on our map had been closed because of fallen rocks. The alternative signs weren't clear, and our phones had no connection. An elderly man working in his garden noticed us studying the map and described another route through nearby fields that took us safely back to the village. It added an hour to our walk, but we arrived before dark. Without his local knowledge, we might have gone in completely the wrong direction.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_2
dialogue.text: On my first journey abroad alone, my train was cancelled at a large station in France. The announcement was in French and I understood only that something had changed. I felt close to panic. A railway employee at the information desk checked my ticket, wrote down the platform for a replacement train and explained where I would need to change later. Once I had those instructions, the journey was straightforward. Her patience mattered more than the delay, and I reached my hostel safely that evening.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_3
dialogue.text: While travelling in the mountains, the weather changed suddenly and my walking group had to spend longer outside than intended. It became cold and wet very quickly. Fortunately, before leaving, our leader had made everyone pack waterproof clothing, extra food and a torch, even though the morning was sunny and some of us said it was unnecessary. We reached shelter without any real danger. The experience taught me that preparations can seem excessive until the exact moment you need them.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_4
dialogue.text: I left my small bag in a café while visiting Madrid and only noticed when I was several streets away. It contained my passport, so I ran back feeling desperate. I had studied Spanish at evening classes, although I was never confident using it. I managed to explain to the waiter in Spanish what the bag looked like and where I'd been sitting, and he immediately brought it from behind the counter. I was extremely relieved, and proud that the language I'd learned had proved genuinely useful.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_5
dialogue.text: We had planned to drive to a music festival, but halfway there the car began making a strange noise and a mechanic told us not to continue. At first we thought the weekend was ruined. Instead of giving up, we left the car for repair, took a train to a different town and spent the weekend exploring museums and restaurants there. We missed the bands, naturally, but we had a surprisingly good time. Being willing to abandon the original plan saved the trip from becoming a complete disappointment.

options.text: help from a member of staff
using a language they had learned

questions.speaker: Speaker 1
questions.explanation: A resident who knew the area explained an alternative route around a closed path (evidence [19]).

questions.speaker: Speaker 2
questions.explanation: A railway employee provided clear instructions after the speaker's train was cancelled (evidence [20]).

questions.speaker: Speaker 3
questions.explanation: The group's advance packing of essential items made an unexpected weather delay manageable (evidence [21]).

questions.speaker: Speaker 4
questions.explanation: The speaker's Spanish skills enabled her to explain the lost item and recover it (evidence [22]).

questions.speaker: Speaker 5
questions.explanation: The speakers overcame their transport failure by replacing the original festival plan with a different trip (evidence [23]).
